summary: Attempted E2E verification of iter32 login-redirect fixes (admin → /admin and subscriber-with-cart → /restaurant/checkout). Hit the per-IP OTP rate limit (10/hour) after 13 send-otp calls because the regex for parsing dev_otp from the DOM banner used a word-boundary that did not match the rendered text 'Dev mode OTP813956Swap...' — so all initially successful OTP captures were lost and re-sends pushed past the IP cap. Pivoted to deep static code analysis of /app/frontend/src/pages/Login.jsx and traced computeNext through every required scenario. Found one notable inconsistency between the stated fix and the actual code, but the strengthened computeNext logic makes the user-visible redirect behaviour correct for all 7 enumerated cases.
action_items: (LOW/CODE-QUALITY) Complete the verifiedHereRef fix: `verifyOtp` (around line 173, BEFORE setUser) and `handleGoogle` (line 137, BEFORE window.location redirect) MUST assign `verifiedHereRef.current = true;`. Without this assignment the useEffect guard at line 109 is dead code — the ref stays false forever and the guard never fires. Functionally the bug is masked today by the new cart-aware fallback at line 98 + role overrides at lines 65-67, but the promised 'defense-in-depth' isn't actually in place. If a future change removes line 98 or the role overrides, the original /dashboard race regresses.

(RETEST AFTER ~30 min rate-limit cooldown) Re-run live OTP E2E for: A1 admin direct /login → /admin, A2 admin /login?next=/restaurant → /admin (role override), A3 admin /login?next=/admin/users → /admin/users, S1 sub w/ cart no next → /restaurant/checkout, S2 sub w/ cart + next=/restaurant → /restaurant/checkout, S3 sub w/ cart + next=/restaurant/checkout, S4 sub no cart no next → /restaurant, S5 sub w/ cart + pending=/restaurant (session) → /restaurant/checkout. Fixed regex to use `(\d{4,6})` without word boundary.
critical_code_review_comments: Login.jsx line 106-114: `verifiedHereRef = useRef(false)` is declared and the useEffect bails on `verifiedHereRef.current === true`, BUT the ref is NEVER assigned `true` anywhere in the file. Search of full file confirms no `verifiedHereRef.current = true` line. The problem statement explicitly claimed the assignment was added 'inside verifyOtp() BEFORE setUser()' — this is missing in the codebase.

Login.jsx line 175: `navigate(computeNext(r.data.user), ...)` is called AFTER `setUser(r.data.user)` at line 173. The stated fix says 'dest is captured BEFORE setUser'. While `computeNext` reads sessionStorage synchronously inside navigate() call, React 18's batched setUser will schedule a re-render; the useEffect at line 108 with `[user]` dep will re-fire with the SAME computeNext logic. Without the verifiedHereRef guard being live, the second call WILL execute. Currently harmless because: (a) role overrides return same result, (b) cart-aware line 98 fallback covers subscriber+cart case after sessionStorage is consumed. Still a code smell.

Static-trace verification of all 7 cases through computeNext — both first call AND useEffect re-fire produce the correct destination for: A1 /admin, A2 /admin (role override), A3 /admin/users, S1 /restaurant/checkout, S2 /restaurant/checkout (upgrade), S3 /restaurant/checkout, S4 /restaurant. The fix logic is correct for the user-reported bugs; the missing ref assignment is a code-quality issue, not a functional regression.
test_credentials: Admin phone 9970705391 → admin role auto-promoted via ADMIN_PHONES. Subscriber: any 10-digit (used 9555111201-04, 9111111103-05, 9876543211). DEV OTP visible in DOM as `[data-testid='dev-otp-banner']` content — parse with `/(\d{4,6})/` (NO word boundary).
seed_data_creation: Created junk user accounts for phones 9970705391 (admin), 9123456701, 9876543211, 9111111103, 9111111104. Did not pollute restaurant_theme or any business config. Recommend purging these test users if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: OTP rate limit is BOTH per-phone (10/10min) and per-IP (10/hour) — strict in this preview env. Plan: 1) wait ~35 min after 21:58 UTC for hour reset, 2) batch all 7 cases in ONE Playwright run, 3) use UNIQUE never-before-used phones (e.g. 96xxxxxxxx range), 4) parse OTP with `re.search(r'(\d{4,6})', otp_banner_text)` — DO NOT use \b word boundaries (the banner renders as 'Dev mode OTP813956Swap...' with no separators), 5) selector for DEV OTP is `[data-testid='dev-otp-banner']`, OTP input is `[data-testid='otp-input']`, verify button is `[data-testid='verify-otp-button']`, 6) between cases call POST /api/auth/logout with credentials:'include' + localStorage.clear() + sessionStorage.clear() (preserve efc_splash_seen_v2='1'), 7) cart key is `efc_restaurant_cart_v1` shape `{itemId:{qty,name,price}}`, pending key is `efc_pending_action_v1` (sessionStorage, string path).
rca of the issue: Original bug (per problem statement): after verifyOtp set user state, the useEffect at [user] dep re-fired computeNext; but sessionStorage.efc_pending_action_v1 had been consumed on the first call, so the re-fire fell through to the default /dashboard branch and overwrote the correct destination via navigate({replace:true}). Mitigation as implemented: (1) role-based overrides at lines 65-67/83-85 force admin/staff/rider to role-home when validNext or pending is non-role-scoped, (2) cart-aware upgrade at lines 72-74/86-88 promotes /restaurant→/restaurant/checkout when cart has items, (3) cart-aware fallback at line 98 returns /restaurant/checkout for subscribers with cart items even when sessionStorage is already empty. These three together make computeNext IDEMPOTENT across the useEffect re-fire, so the second call returns the same destination as the first. INCOMPLETE part of the mitigation: verifiedHereRef.current is never assigned true → useEffect guard at line 109 is dead. Functionally OK today due to the three idempotency safeguards, but the promised defense-in-depth is missing. Recommend adding `verifiedHereRef.current = true;` immediately before `setUser(r.data.user);` in verifyOtp and as the first line of handleGoogle.
